markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Emit EventLedger/Flight Recorder events for view open, search, link create/delete, tag, pin, projection rebuild, and repair. This MT belongs to LoomObsidianNavigation. Deliver the Obsidian replacement surface as a Handshake-native Loom projection.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Obsidian-like behavior must be implemented as Loom/project knowledge projections over PostgreSQL, not a markdown-vault clone.
Backlinks, graph, tags, folders, pins, color labels, and project links must have backend navigation APIs and visual-debug evidence.
scope.expected_behavior: LoomEventLedgerEvents leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: LoomEventLedgerEvents is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence.summary: Completed FR-EVT-LOOM event coverage for the Loom surface. Pre-existing Loom FR events: LoomBlockCreated/Updated/Deleted, LoomEdgeCreated/Deleted (link+tag), LoomViewQueried (view open), LoomSearchExecuted, LoomDedupHit, LoomPreviewGenerated, LoomAiTag{Suggested,Accepted,Rejected}. MT-177 binds block indexing (KNOWLEDGE_LOOM_BLOCK_INDEXED) to the EventLedger. This MT adds the missing projection-rebuild event.
files_changed: src/backend/handshake_core/src/flight_recorder/mod.rs - new LoomProjectionRebuilt event type + Display (loom_projection_rebuilt) + validate_loom_projection_rebuilt_payload (exact keys: type/workspace_id/projection_id/operation{compile,regenerate}/source_block_count) wired into the validate dispatch; +2 unit tests

src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/loom.rs - compile + regenerate wiki projection now emit a VALID loom_projection_rebuilt FR event (the compile handler previously emitted a loom_view_queried-typed payload that the recorder validator would have REJECTED - a latent bug fixed here)

tests: flight_recorder_loom_projection_rebuilt_valid - compile + regenerate payloads validate; Display == loom_projection_rebuilt
flight_recorder_loom_projection_rebuilt_rejects_bad_payload - bad operation value, missing projection_id, and wrong type string all rejected

evidence: Status flip note (2026-06-12T06:25:27.785Z): Loom engine group backend-complete per delegate report - real-PG proven (isolated schemas, full migration chain), per-MT adversarial review recorded in this file. SQLite removed/unreachable (orphans git-rm'd, sqlx has no sqlite feature, source regression guard added). Markdown-vault-never-authority proven via projection-delete byte-identity negative test. Stable /workspaces/:ws/loom/* HTTP contracts documented for the Notes UI. Implementation commit(s): 89476e5c.

findings: LATENT INVALID-EVENT (fixed): the MT-184 compile handler emitted a LoomViewQueried event whose payload (type=loom_wiki_projection_compiled, no view_type) would FAIL the loom_view_queried validator -> the event would silently drop (let _ = record). Replaced with a dedicated, validator-backed LoomProjectionRebuilt event.
EXHAUSTIVENESS (verified): the new enum variant is handled in Display + the validate dispatch; no category/severity mapping mis-buckets it (build is green, exhaustive matches updated).
PAYLOAD STRICTNESS (handled+tested): require_exact_keys + require_limited_choice(operation) + require_safe_id_string reject malformed payloads, matching the discipline of the other Loom validators.

COVERAGE (verified): view open/search/link create+delete/tag/pin(block updated)/dedup/preview already have FR event types; projection rebuild is now covered; 'repair' is a frontend/queue surface with no backend Loom op in this group.
fixes: Add LoomProjectionRebuilt event type + validator; emit it (valid payload) from compile and regenerate instead of the invalid LoomViewQueried event.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-190 requires EventLedger/Flight Recorder event coverage for the Loom surface (view open, search, link create/delete, tag, pin, projection rebuild, repair). The implementation adds the one missing event type (LoomProjectionRebuilt) with a strict validator, fixes a real latent bug where the compile handler emitted an invalid LoomViewQueried-typed payload that the recorder would have silently dropped, and wires it into two live PostgreSQL-backed HTTP handlers. Proof exercises the real production validate() dispatch with valid and negative cases. Code implements the MT behavior with real resource touch and no placeholders.
findings: commit 89476e5c adds FlightRecorderEventType::LoomProjectionRebuilt (flight_recorder/mod.rs:181), Display arm (mod.rs:392), validate dispatch arm (mod.rs:1007), and validator validate_loom_projection_rebuilt_payload (mod.rs:~2665) using real production helpers require_exact_keys / require_fixed_string / require_safe_id_string / require_limited_choice / require_number (all confirmed present at mod.rs 1194/4751/2387/2396/1309).

Real latent-bug fix verified: api/loom.rs compile_loom_wiki_projection (now line 850-877) previously emitted FlightRecorderEventType::LoomViewQueried with payload type=loom_wiki_projection_compiled, which would fail the loom_view_queried validator and silently drop via 'let _ = record_event'. It now emits a VALID LoomProjectionRebuilt event. regenerate_loom_wiki_projection (line 919-945) had no event at all and now emits one (operation=regenerate).

Both emission sites are on live HTTP routes: POST /workspaces/:workspace_id/loom/wiki -> compile (api/loom.rs:153-154) and the :projection_id regenerate route; both call real PostgreSQL-backed state.storage.compile/regenerate_loom_wiki_projection and record through the real state.flight_recorder. Not dead code.

sub_rule_2 satisfied: the durable resource (FlightRecorder/EventLedger event type + strict validator) is exercised through the real production event.validate() dispatch in tests flight_recorder_loom_projection_rebuilt_valid and _rejects_bad_payload (mod.rs:6987+), including 3 negative cases (bad operation, missing projection_id, wrong type string). No implementer-authored mock substitutes for the real validator.

sub_rule_1 satisfied: no todo!/unimplemented!/deferred-live escape reachable from the MT path in api/loom.rs or the new flight_recorder code. The 5 unreachable! hits (mod.rs:6344-6400) are test-setup as_object_mut() guards, unrelated to MT-190.

Spec alignment: the canonical FR-EVT-LOOM table (spec module 11, lines 856-867) enumerates 001-012 and does NOT list projection-rebuild; however spec module 02 line 2719 explicitly requires 'projection rebuild' + 'EventLedger replay' as a WP-KERNEL-009 vertical-slice proof. loom_projection_rebuilt is a Handshake-native extension event satisfying that requirement; it does not redefine or contradict any 001-012 event. ALIGNED.

Aside (non-blocking, paperwork): MT contract lifecycle.adversarial_code_review is a KERNEL_BUILDER self-review and carries no authority over this independent review; lifecycle.claimed_by is null so sub_rule_3 self-cert is NA.
evidence_checked: src/backend/handshake_core/src/flight_recorder/mod.rs (enum 168-181, Display 380-392, validate dispatch 973-1007, validator ~2662, tests 6984+, helper fns 1194/1309/2387/2396/4751)
.GOV/spec/master-spec-v02.194/spec-modules/02-system-architecture.md (line 2719 vertical-slice proof, 2690 projection rebuild failure receipts)

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
